summary: Code review testing for MiraDemoPage.jsx refactoring (Stage 1: usePet hook integration). Browser automation crashes due to page size (~6000 lines) as warned by main agent. Code compiles successfully with only ESLint warnings.

notes: Browser automation tool crashes when testing MiraDemoPage due to its ~6000 line size - this is a known limitation
ESLint warnings for missing dependencies in useEffect/useCallback (react-hooks/exhaustive-deps) - these are non-blocking

critical_code_review_comments: PASS: usePet hook correctly imported from '../hooks/mira' (line 41)
PASS: Hook destructures pet, setPet, allPets, setAllPets, showPetSelector, setShowPetSelector, isLoadingPets, isRealPet (lines 645-654)
PASS: DEMO_PET and ALL_DEMO_PETS exported from usePet.js with correct default values - 'Buddy' as name, 'Golden Retriever' as breed
PASS: Local switchPet function intentionally kept separate from hook's switchPet due to additional session management logic (lines 1576-1630)
PASS: Comments indicate pet state now comes from hook (lines 641-644, 678, 854)
PASS: No duplicate variable declarations - previous compilation errors resolved
PASS: Frontend webpack compiles successfully with only warnings (react-hooks/exhaustive-deps)
PASS: Backend API health check returns healthy status

success_rate.frontend: Code review: 100% (compilation successful, hook integration correct)

test_credentials: No login required for demo mode
context_for_next_testing_agent: MiraDemoPage.jsx is ~6000 lines and crashes browser automation tools. Use curl/API testing or code review instead. The usePet hook is correctly integrated and the page compiles without errors.
limitations: Browser automation testing not possible due to page size crashing Playwright. Code review and compilation verification performed instead.
